with_plugin.note: rt-11 failure is test-order contamination (CRUD test created entity before uninstall), not a real code defect

adjusted_note: WITH rt-11 failure is spurious (entity content blocked uninstall, not code error). Adjusted: WITH=14/16 (87.5%) vs WITHOUT=12/16 (75.0%)

expectation_breakdown.name: _csrf_token on state-changing route
expectation_breakdown.note: shared failure — neither variant adds CSRF protection

expectation_breakdown.name: Entity handlers array includes access key
Access handler registered in entity type

details.name: restrict access on dangerous perms
details.why_with_passed: WITH variant's PermissionProvider.php includes 'restrict access' => TRUE on edit_any and delete_any permissions. access-security skill teaches this pattern.
details.why_without_failed: WITHOUT variant's PermissionCallbacks.php generates delete_any/edit_any but omits restrict access flag entirely.

details.name: Entity CRUD works end-to-end (runtime)
details.why_with_passed: WITH variant entity CRUD works — correct namespace references throughout.
details.why_without_failed: WITHOUT variant uses Drupal\system\Entity\User::getCurrentUserId (wrong namespace) causing fatal TypeError. Correct: Drupal\user\Entity\User.

shared_failures.name: _csrf_token on state-changing route
shared_failures.note: Neither variant adds CSRF protection. This pattern is in drupal-access-security SKILL.md but Haiku doesn't pick it up in this context. Potential skill iteration target.

runtime_value.note: Runtime assertions caught a fatal bug (wrong User class namespace) that static analysis would have missed. This validates the ddev-based grading approach.
